description:
## Background
The App startup load and workspace-transition subscription originally depended on `[refreshWorkspaceState]` and `[applyTransition]`. The migration replaced both with `useMountEffect`, whose implementation always passes `[]`. That erases the reactive contract and leaves those asynchronous closures bound to the first callback identities if either dependency ever changes.

## Evaluation and plan
Add a clearly named dependency-aware `useExternalLifecycle` helper alongside `useMountEffect`. Both helpers may centralize the one intentionally suppressed underlying React effect, but their interfaces must make the lifecycle contract explicit: `useMountEffect` remains mount-only, while `useExternalLifecycle(effect, dependencies)` re-runs and cleans up when its dependency list changes. Restore the original dependency arrays at the App call sites: `[refreshWorkspaceState]` for initial issue/workspace loading and `[applyTransition]` for native workspace-transition subscription.

Do not solve this by disabling lint at App call sites or by relying on the current `useCallback` identities remaining stable. Keep asynchronous listener registration disposal-safe and preserve startup transition-gate ordering, failure handling, and listener cleanup. Add a focused helper test proving rerun/cleanup on dependency changes; retain existing App startup and workspace-transition tests.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] App no longer uses mount-only semantics for effects that have reactive dependencies.
- [ ] The startup load rebinds/re-runs when `refreshWorkspaceState` changes, matching its original dependency contract.
- [ ] The workspace-transition listener is removed and re-registered when `applyTransition` changes, with late registration still disposed after unmount.
- [ ] `useMountEffect` remains correct for genuinely mount-only lifecycles.
- [ ] No direct `useEffect` call or call-site suppression is introduced outside the centralized lifecycle helper.
- [ ] Focused App/helper tests cover dependency reruns, cleanup, stale callback avoidance, and existing transition behavior.
- [ ] Typecheck, lint, and focused tests pass.

## Verification
- `pnpm exec vitest run src/App.workspace-switching.test.tsx src/App.workspace-recovery.test.tsx src/lib/use-external-lifecycle.test.tsx`
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check`

Triage: planned for implementation on `bsm-gbd/eliminate-direct-useeffect`.
title: Preserve App lifecycle effect dependency semantics
